# Alt Behavior for Widget Tool Menus

## Problem

Widget tool menus (overflow `...` menu and dropdown menus) only support a single action per entry. Users need a secondary "alt" behavior accessible by holding the Alt/Option key ŌĆö useful for power-user shortcuts like "Copy widget ID" instead of "Copy link to widget".

## Approach

Add an `alt` property to the widget config schema that any menu item can use. When present:
- A muted `Ōīź Alt label` hint appears on the far right of the menu item
- While the user holds Alt, the menu label swaps to the alt label for all entries that have alts
- Clicking while Alt is held triggers the alt action instead of the normal action

First use case: `Copy link to widget` ŌåÆ Alt: `Copy widget ID` (copies `{pathname}/{widgetId}` instead of the full URL with query params).

## Files to Change

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `packages/core/widgets.config.json` | Add `alt` objects to `copy-link` features, add `label:copy-id` variable |
| `packages/react/src/canvas/widgets/widgetConfig.js` | Update `resolveFeature` to resolve variables inside nested `alt` objects |
| `packages/react/src/canvas/widgets/WidgetChrome.jsx` | Add `useAltKey` hook, update `WidgetOverflowMenu` and `DropdownFeature` to support alt behavior, handle `copy-widget-id` action |
| `packages/react/src/canvas/widgets/WidgetChrome.module.css` | Add styles for alt hint text (muted, right-aligned) |

## Steps

### 1. Config: Add alt schema to `widgets.config.json`
- Add variable: `"label:copy-id": "Copy widget ID"`
- Add `"alt": { "label": "$label:copy-id", "action": "copy-widget-id" }` to every `copy-link` feature entry (all 7 widget types)

### 2. Config loader: Resolve alt variables in `widgetConfig.js`
- In `resolveFeature()`, if a feature has an `alt` object, resolve `$variable` refs inside it too

### 3. WidgetChrome: Add `useAltKey()` hook
- Listens for `keydown`/`keyup` on the Alt key
- Returns boolean `altHeld` state
- Resets on window blur (prevent stuck state)

### 4. WidgetChrome: Update `WidgetOverflowMenu`
- Use `useAltKey()` hook
- For each menu item with `.alt`:
  - Default: show normal label + muted `Ōīź {alt.label}` hint on the far right
  - Alt held: swap entire label to alt label, hide the hint
- On click: dispatch `feature.alt.action` when alt is held, otherwise normal action
- Handle `copy-widget-id` action: copies `{pathname}/{widgetId}`

### 5. WidgetChrome: Update `DropdownFeature`
- Same alt rendering logic for dropdown items that have `.alt`

### 6. CSS: Alt hint styles
- `.altHint` ŌĆö muted color, `margin-left: auto`, smaller font
- `.overflowItemAlt` ŌĆö visual feedback when alt label is active

## Edge Cases & Risks

- **Alt key stuck after blur**: Reset alt state on window blur
- **Keyboard focus vs mouse**: Alt key is global, works regardless of mouse position
- **macOS Option key**: `e.key === 'Alt'` works for both Alt and Option
- **Production visibility**: `copy-link` features have `"prod": true`, so alt behavior must work in prod too ŌĆö no filtering needed since `alt` is a sub-property of an already-visible feature
- **No alt on touch devices**: Alt key doesn't exist on touch ŌĆö hint is purely a keyboard enhancement, not required for basic functionality
